ogTitle: Raymond Terrace vs Razorback: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Raymond Terrace and Razorback.
heading: Raymond Terrace vs Razorback — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Raymond Terrace, New South Wales and Razorback, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Raymond Terrace has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Razorback (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Raymond Terrace or Razorback safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Raymond Terrace has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Razorback scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Raymond Terrace scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Razorback scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Raymond Terrace or Razorback?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Raymond Terrace has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
